note: All findings live under install-sizes-calc/C-based/tdnf-size-estimate, a fork of github.com/vmware/tdnf with a size-estimate command added. Real bugs were patched in-tree; remaining warnings/notes inherit from the upstream tdnf project and are accepted on the same posture as upstream.

accepted.reason: tdnf's transaction history is intentionally stored as a SQLite database on /var/lib/tdnf and is consulted by ordinary tdnf operations. Encrypting it would break the package manager.
tdnf-config is a CLI helper; the filename argument names the config file to rewrite. rename() of the operator-supplied target is the documented contract.
Same CLI tool; unlink of the operator-supplied path in the error cleanup path.
Same CLI tool; unlink of the operator-supplied path.
TDNFJoinArrayToString pre-computes nSize = sum(strlen(ppszArray[i])) + nSepSize*(count-1) + 1 and TDNFAllocates pszResult to nSize before the strcpy. The destination is provably large enough.
TDNFJoinArrayToStringSorted pre-computes nSize over the sorted line set before allocating pszResult; strcpy targets are within the allocated buffer.
Same TDNFJoinArrayToStringSorted; same pre-computed nSize guarantee.
prv_pkgname is the caller-owned previous-package-name buffer (sized by caller in tdnf's solver loop); pkgname is a fixed-size local copy of the package name parsed from solver output.
The 'credentials' are a pytest fixture for an in-process HTTPS test repo. Not real credentials and not exposed outside the test harness.
Pytest fixture; disables verification against the self-signed cert that the test harness generates seconds earlier in the same process.
Pytest fixture creating an HTTPS test server. Upstream tdnf uses this idiom for its python test harness; not production code.

hard_fixes.fix: Added NULL guard on strrchr(beg, '"') before *end = '\0'. If the closing quote is missing, the os-release line is skipped instead of dereferencing NULL.

Set value = NULL after free(value) on the error path. Although the cleanup label currently does not re-free, the explicit NULL assignment prevents a future double-free regression and silences the analyzer.
Same: set value = NULL after the normal-path free(value).

Rewrote 'p = buf + strlen(buf) - 1' to first check strnlen(buf, sizeof(buf)) > 0. Empty buf no longer creates a (buf - 1) pointer underflow. Snyk continues to flag the bounded 'buf + blen - 1' on the post-patch line 73 (and apparently double-counts it); blen is bounded by sizeof(buf) (a stack array), so the arithmetic cannot overflow size_t. Accepting the residual as FALSE-POSITIVE.
